title: Lycoctonum longe-crassidatum
properties: Lycoctonum longe-crassidatum, also known as the long-stemmed lycoctonum, has been traditionally used in herbal medicine for its therapeutic properties. The plant is said to possess anti-inflammatory and antiseptic properties, which may help in wound healing and soothing irritated skin. Its leaves and stems are rich in flavonoids and phenolic acids, contributing to its potential medicinal benefits. However, more research is needed to confirm the plant's efficacy and safety for human use. The plant is primarily found in the mountainous regions of the Sichuan province in China.

intro: Lycoctonum longe-crassidatum, also known as Aconitum longe-crassidatum or Long-spurred Monk's Hood, is a species of flowering plant in the family Ranunculaceae. This plant has been traditionally used in medicine to treat fever, rheumatism, and other inflammatory conditions due to its analgesic, anti-inflammatory, and antipyretic properties. The bioactive constituents of Lycoctonum longe-crassidatum include alkaloids, flavonoids, and glycosides, including aconitine, mesaconitine, and hypaconitine, which contribute to its medicinal properties. Lycoctonum longe-crassidatum is commonly used in traditional Chinese medicine and other herbal preparations, such as decoctions, infusions, and tinctures, although it should be handled with caution due to its toxic properties.
